h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner follows the tutor's step-by-step layout check and copying sequence without departure.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: No new device, criterion, or task is performed; the learner only confirms the matching and states readiness to copy.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner pressure or breakdown occurs; the tutor never adapts, only continues the same layout-and-copy routine.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No learner pivot exists, so no contingent tutor uptake can follow.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: No new device, criterion, or task is performed; the learner only confirms the matching and states readiness to copy.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No learner pivot exists, so no contingent tutor uptake can follow.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner pressure or breakdown occurs; the tutor never adapts, only continues the same layout-and-copy routine.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner pressure or breakdown occurs; the tutor never adapts, only continues the same layout-and-copy routine.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue placing labels according to the tutor's 'object on the cart' format without reinterpreting the activity.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Table on the cart, for the friction contact. The arrow direction checks which force it is, but the label is the object acting on the cart.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L3 independently applies the object-on-the-cart naming convention to the friction arrow and explicitly states the criterion differentiating arrow direction from force identity, performing the labeling task with a new reflexive criterion.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates visible pressure that breaks the tutor's prior moves; the tutor's consistent 'object on the cart' scaffolding continues smoothly without a reversal.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is introduced; the tutor maintains the same object-naming framing throughout.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: After the pivot L1, the tutor simply continues the established 'object on the cart' routine without adapting to any revised learner framing.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Table on the cart, for the friction contact. The arrow direction checks which force it is, but the label is the object acting on the cart.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: L3 independently applies the object-on-the-cart naming convention to the friction arrow and explicitly states the criterion differentiating arrow direction from force identity, performing the labeling task with a new reflexive criterion.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: After the pivot L1, the tutor simply continues the established 'object on the cart' routine without adapting to any revised learner framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner turn creates visible pressure that breaks the tutor's prior moves; the tutor's consistent 'object on the cart' scaffolding continues smoothly without a reversal.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner turn creates visible pressure that breaks the tutor's prior moves; the tutor's consistent 'object on the cart' scaffolding continues smoothly without a reversal.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: No adaptive mechanism is introduced; the tutor maintains the same object-naming framing throughout.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will move from repeating the correct decomposition of the time signature into doing the physical worksheet task (drawing the bar line, placing cards).
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: draws the bar line where the acetate strip sits.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner performs the requested worksheet action (drawing the bar line) but this is a simple confirmation of the ongoing procedural task, not a new device, criterion, or productive movement in the learning problem.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure that reveals a failed teaching route; the tutor never takes stock or invents a new adaptive learning mechanism.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn and thus no later tutor uptake adapting to a revised learner framing.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: draws the bar line where the acetate strip sits.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner performs the requested worksheet action (drawing the bar line) but this is a simple confirmation of the ongoing procedural task, not a new device, criterion, or productive movement in the learning problem.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn and thus no later tutor uptake adapting to a revised learner framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner turn creates pressure that reveals a failed teaching route; the tutor never takes stock or invents a new adaptive learning mechanism.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner turn creates pressure that reveals a failed teaching route; the tutor never takes stock or invents a new adaptive learning mechanism.
